---
title: "Process spatial samples"
description: "The core processing flow, adapted for spatial data."
categories: [openpipeline_spatial]
order: 35
---

[`openpipeline_spatial`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_spatial}<span class="swatch openpipeline_spatial"></span>

`spatial_process_samples`, from **`openpipeline_spatial`**, runs the same
per-sample and cohort steps as core [sample processing](process-samples.qmd),
filtering, normalization, feature selection, PCA, but adapted for spatial data
and keeping the per-spot coordinates in `.obsm["spatial"]` intact.

## Options

| Workflow | Use when |
|---|---|
| [`multiomics/spatial_process_samples`](../reference/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/workflows/multiomics/spatial_process_samples.qmd) | Processing a [spatially-ingested](ingest-spatial.qmd) cohort |

## Example: process a spatial cohort

For example, on a [`param_list`](process-many-samples.qmd) of spatial `.h5mu`
files:

```yaml
# params.yaml
param_list:
  - id: sample_A
    input: results/ingestion/sample_A.h5mu
  - id: sample_B
    input: results/ingestion/sample_B.h5mu
publish_dir: results/processed
```

```bash
nextflow run https://packages.viash-hub.com/vsh/openpipeline_spatial.git \
  -r v0.5.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/multiomics/spatial_process_samples/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

## How it works

`spatial_process_samples` runs the same two phases as core
[sample processing](process-samples.qmd), minus doublet detection (spatial spots
aren't droplets, so Scrublet doesn't apply).

**Per sample, before concatenation.** Each sample is filtered on its own
distribution:

- **Count-based**: drop spots/cells whose total counts or detected features fall
  outside a defined range.
- **Quantile-based**: derive thresholds from the data's own distribution rather
  than fixed cutoffs.
- **Feature-based**: keep or drop cells based on specific features or feature sets.
- **Mitochondrial / ribosomal fraction**: remove cells with a high fraction of
  mitochondrial or ribosomal counts.

**Across the cohort, after concatenation.** Samples are concatenated (a
`sample_id` column records each cell's origin), then processed together:
log-normalization, optional scaling, highly variable gene (HVG) selection, and
PCA. Throughout, the per-spot coordinates in `.obsm["spatial"]` are carried
untouched.

## Key choices

- Thresholds work as in core [sample processing](process-samples.qmd); set them
  from a [spatial QC report](qc-report-spatial.qmd).
- Coordinates in `.obsm["spatial"]` are carried through untouched, downstream
  spatial steps (e.g. [niche identification](spatial-niches.qmd)) depend on them.

## Output

One merged, processed object (`merged.spatial_process_samples.output.h5mu`) with
normalized counts, a PCA embedding, and the spatial coordinates preserved, ready
for [integration](integrate.qmd).
